purpose: Follow-up roadmap that builds on todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json and adds CodeCompass-specific hybrid retrieval capabilities: structured CodeCompass outputs, FTS/BM25, embedding/vector search, graph expansion and relation-aware context assembly for worker execution. This file is intentionally a second todo and must not replace the worker sandbox balanced roadmap.

relevant_parent_milestones: WSB-M6 Incremental retrieval and planning loop
WSB-M8 Advanced indexing and retrieval parity
WSB-M9 Advanced planning and execution control
relevant_parent_tasks: WSB-T19 Embedding provider abstraction and index contract add
WSB-T20 Incremental repository indexing strategy implement
WSB-T21 Budgeted retrieval and reranking pipeline integrate
WSB-T27 Hybrid retrieval architecture contract define
WSB-T28 Language-aware and AST-assisted chunking strategy implement
WSB-T29 Commit-aware incremental index invalidation and rebuild add
WSB-T30 Hybrid retrieval merge and rank calibration implement
WSB-T31 Reranker integration and query rewriting pipeline add
WSB-T32 Retrieval evaluation harness and benchmark corpus add
WSB-T33 Retrieval SLO guardrails and cache strategy add
parent_todo.rule: This todo may depend on and refine WSB retrieval tasks, but it must not duplicate or rewrite the worker profile, sandbox, policy, approval, standalone runtime or command execution tasks.

related_todo_files.handling_rule: Do not edit or override worker execution contracts here. This file adds CodeCompass retrieval detail on top.

related_todo_files.role: detailed hub-side CodeCompass hybrid retrieval roadmap
related_todo_files.handling_rule: Reuse its retrieval-engine details where possible. This worker follow-up defines how those capabilities are consumed by the worker sandbox flow.
Do not copy these tasks into main todo until parent worker track stabilizes.

goal: Make the worker use CodeCompass as a high-quality context source without moving retrieval ownership into the worker execution backend. CodeCompass should improve worker task success through structured context, exact lexical hits, semantic search and graph-based neighborhood expansion.
core_principle: The worker receives bounded, explainable context. It does not own global repository indexing, graph traversal policy or retrieval governance unless explicitly running in standalone mode with the same contracts.

hub_mode: CodeCompass outputs feed FTS/BM25, vector and graph engines.
ContextBundleService creates compact grounded prompt plus provenance.
Worker sandbox receives task-local context and executes plan/patch/test/verify.
Worker returns artifacts/results/trace; hub records retrieval provenance and execution trace.
standalone_worker_mode: Standalone worker receives workspace plus control manifest.
Standalone worker may build or load local CodeCompass-derived indexes inside sandbox.
Same contracts for chunks, vectors, FTS records, graph edges and provenance apply.
Standalone output includes retrieval trace, context manifest and artifacts.

codecompass_rag_helper: Does not decide worker execution policy.
retrieval_layer: Normalizes relation edges and performs graph expansion.
Scores, deduplicates, reranks and budgets context.
worker_layer: Produces diffs, artifacts, verification outputs and degraded reasons.
Does not silently fetch unbounded repository context.

current_state_assumptions: todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json already contains generic worker retrieval/indexing tasks WSB-T19..WSB-T33.
Ananta already has a hub-side ContextManagerService/RagService/RetrievalService path for grounded prompts.
KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService already reads CodeCompass index.jsonl, details.jsonl and relations.jsonl with custom lexical scoring.
SemanticSearchEngine already exists through LlamaIndex, but not necessarily as structured CodeCompass embedding.jsonl indexing.
CodeCompass graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl are generated by rag-helper but are not yet clearly used for graph traversal in retrieval.
The worker sandbox track is active, so this roadmap must avoid broad worker contract churn.
non_goals: Do not move hub-owned policy, approval, routing or audit into CodeCompass.
Do not require external embedding services for worker startup.
Do not force OpenCode or ananta-worker to implement their own repository retrieval.
Do not remove existing lexical KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService scoring before benchmarks prove the replacement is better.
Do not send full repository dumps to the worker as a shortcut.
Do not make graph expansion unbounded or opaque.
Do not break existing use_hybrid_context=true execution path.
success_criteria: Worker sandbox receives CodeCompass-enriched context through the existing context bundle path.
CodeCompass FTS/BM25 finds exact classes, methods, annotations, XML tags, SQL names, errors and config keys.
CodeCompass vector search indexes embedding.jsonl as structured records with metadata preserved.
Graph expansion can add bounded parent/child/call/dependency/JPA/Spring/XML neighborhood context.
Relation-aware context explains why adjacent records were selected.
Worker context budgets prevent graph/vector retrieval from flooding prompts.
Fallback works when FTS, vector or graph engines are missing/degraded.
Benchmarks compare baseline knowledge_index scoring against FTS, vector, graph and full hybrid modes.
All output is traceable: task_id, trace_id, context_hash, selected engines, source records and expansion path.
The parent worker sandbox track can continue independently without this file changing its core safety model.

milestones.title: CodeCompass output contracts for worker retrieval
Structured vector retrieval over CodeCompass embeddings

tasks.title: Map CodeCompass follow-up to worker sandbox tasks
acceptance_criteria: Document exactly which WSB tasks this follow-up extends.
State that this file is a second todo and not a replacement for todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json.
Map CodeCompass FTS/vector/graph work to WSB retrieval/indexing tasks.
Identify which tasks are hub-mode only and which are relevant to standalone worker mode.

tasks.title: Define worker-safe CodeCompass ownership boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Define what the hub retrieval layer owns in hub mode.
Define what standalone worker mode may own locally.
Explicitly forbid execution backends from owning global retrieval policy.
Document allowed integration seams: ContextBundleService, RetrievalService, worker context manifest.

tasks.title: Add CodeCompass retrieval feature flag matrix
acceptance_criteria: Flags exist for codecompass_fts, codecompass_vector, codecompass_graph and codecompass_relation_expansion.
Each flag can be enabled independently.
Default behavior remains safe and does not require external embedding services.
Flag diagnostics show disabled, ready, degraded and missing_dependency states.
Tests prove worker execution still starts when all CodeCompass flags are disabled.

tasks.title: Define CodeCompass context trust and redaction rules
acceptance_criteria: Document that CodeCompass-derived context is untrusted input to the worker prompt.
Prompt-injection-like instructions inside comments, docs, XML or generated context are treated as data, not commands.
Sensitive tokens in CodeCompass-derived content are redacted before worker prompts.
Tests cover hostile comments embedded in retrieved source snippets.

tasks.title: Create CodeCompass output manifest contract for worker retrieval
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines available CodeCompass output files and their hashes/mtimes.
Schema supports index, details, context, embedding, relations, graph_nodes and graph_edges JSONL files.
Schema includes codecompass_version, profile_name, source_scope and generated_at fields.
Tests validate complete, partial and missing-output manifests.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass output reader shared by hub and standalone worker
acceptance_criteria: Reader can load CodeCompass outputs from a hub KnowledgeIndex output_dir.
Reader can load CodeCompass outputs from a standalone worker workspace path.
Reader tolerates missing optional outputs and malformed JSONL lines with degraded diagnostics.
Reader yields records with consistent provenance metadata.
No hub database import is required for standalone reader use.

tasks.title: Normalize CodeCompass records into retrieval documents
acceptance_criteria: Normalize index/details/context/embedding/relations records into a common RetrievalDocument shape.
Preserve record_id, kind, file, parent_id, role_labels, importance_score and generated_code when present.
Produce separate text fields for symbol, path, summary, content, relation and focus text.
Deterministic document hash is stable across identical records.

tasks.title: Add CodeCompass cache invalidation metadata
acceptance_criteria: Cache state includes output manifest hash, CodeCompass profile, retrieval engine version and embedding model version.
Cache invalidates when output files or model versions change.
Cache invalidation is independent for FTS, vector and graph stores.
Tests cover changed manifest, changed embedding model and deleted output file.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass FTS/BM25 storage
acceptance_criteria: Use SQLite FTS5 when available.
Index normalized fields: symbol_text, path_text, kind_text, summary_text, content_text, relation_text, focus_text.
Store source metadata outside the FTS table for stable provenance.
Support rebuild and incremental refresh based on CodeCompass cache state.
Report degraded state when FTS5 is unavailable without failing worker startup.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass exact-symbol query parsing
acceptance_criteria: Parser detects Java class names, method names, fully qualified names, XML tag names, property keys and error tokens.
Parser emits exact-symbol, phrase and broad-token query forms.
Parser does not execute or trust query content as commands.
Tests cover Java, Spring, JPA, XML, YAML/properties and stacktrace queries.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass FTS retrieval engine
acceptance_criteria: Engine returns ContextChunk-compatible records with engine='codecompass_fts'.
BM25 score and field boost breakdown are included in metadata.
Exact symbol/path hits are boosted above broad content hits.
Engine supports task_kind and retrieval_intent weighting.
Tests verify expected ranking for exact class, method, annotation, config key and XML tag queries.

tasks.title: Wire CodeCompass FTS into worker retrieval pipeline as optional channel
acceptance_criteria: FTS channel can be enabled independently in worker retrieval config.
When disabled/unavailable, worker retrieval falls back to existing lexical/symbol channels.
Selected FTS chunks appear in retrieval trace and context bundle provenance.
Tests verify no backend execution contract changes are required.

tasks.title: Implement structured embedding.jsonl loader for vector indexing
acceptance_criteria: Loader uses embedding_text as primary vector text.
Loader preserves structured metadata rather than treating JSONL as one raw document.
Records without embedding_text are skipped with diagnostic count.
Metadata includes id, kind, file, parent_id, role_labels, importance_score, source_scope and profile_name when available.

tasks.title: Implement swappable embedding provider for CodeCompass vectors
acceptance_criteria: Embedding provider abstraction supports fake/test provider, local provider and OpenAI-compatible endpoint configuration.
No external embedding service is required for tests.
Embedding model name and dimension are recorded in index metadata.
Provider failures degrade vector channel without failing FTS or graph retrieval.

tasks.title: Implement structured CodeCompass vector store
acceptance_criteria: Vector store indexes one vector per CodeCompass embedding record.
Vector store preserves record metadata for later graph expansion.
Vector store supports rebuild when embedding model version or output manifest changes.
Vector store exposes top_k search with scores and source metadata.
Tests use deterministic fake vectors.

tasks.title: Add CodeCompass vector retrieval channel
acceptance_criteria: Engine returns ContextChunk-compatible records with engine='codecompass_vector'.
Vector metadata includes record_id, kind, file, vector_score, model_name and source manifest hash.
Task profiles can boost vector results for fuzzy semantic queries and architecture analysis.
Vector channel is optional and has clear degraded diagnostics.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass graph store loader
acceptance_criteria: Load graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl when present.
Index nodes by id, file, kind, name/symbol and record_id when available.
Index outgoing and incoming edges by node id and edge type.
Protect traversal against cycles and oversized expansions.
Missing graph files degrade graph channel without disabling FTS/vector retrieval.

tasks.title: Normalize relations.jsonl into graph edges
acceptance_criteria: Normalize relation records into edge type, source id, target id/resolved target, confidence and provenance.

Support relations such as child_of_file, child_of_type, declares_method, calls_probable_target, injects_dependency, field_type_uses, extends, implements, JPA relations, transactional_boundary and declares_bean.
Unresolved relations are kept as low-confidence textual relation candidates.
Tests cover resolved, unresolved and malformed relation records.

tasks.title: Implement bounded graph expansion profiles
acceptance_criteria: Implement expansion profiles: bugfix_local, refactor_navigation, architecture_review, config_integration.
Each profile defines allowed edge types, max depth, max nodes and per-kind budget.
Method seeds can expand to parent type, called probable targets and used types.
Type seeds can expand to file parent, important members, injected dependencies and extends/implements.
Config/XML seeds can expand to related runtime/config edges when available.
Expansion is deterministic and bounded.

tasks.title: Add graph-expanded context chunk builder
acceptance_criteria: Graph expansion returns compact context chunks, not raw full graph dumps.
Each chunk includes expanded_from, relation_path and expansion_reason metadata.
Content is clipped and redacted before prompt assembly.
Tests verify source seed and expanded neighbor grouping.

tasks.title: Add relation-aware context explanation strings
acceptance_criteria: Generate short explanations such as selected because this method calls target X or selected because this class injects Y.
Explanations are machine-readable and human-readable.
Explanations are included in retrieval trace and optionally in context bundle metadata.
No explanation includes huge raw source blobs.

tasks.title: Extend worker context bundle contract with CodeCompass provenance
acceptance_criteria: Context bundle schema supports codecompass provenance fields.
Fields include engine, record_id, file, kind, score, expanded_from, relation_path and manifest_hash.
Workers can consume context_text without understanding all metadata.
Metadata is preserved for trace/audit.

tasks.title: Integrate CodeCompass channels into worker retrieval merge
acceptance_criteria: Merge supports codecompass_fts, codecompass_vector and codecompass_graph channels.
Scores are normalized across channels.
Deduplication merges same record from FTS/vector/graph without losing provenance.
Graph expansion happens after seed retrieval and before final context budgeting.
Final ranking includes channel contribution breakdown.

tasks.title: Apply worker profile budgets to CodeCompass retrieval
acceptance_criteria: safe, balanced and fast profiles map to different retrieval depth and expansion budgets.
safe profile favors exact and minimal context.
balanced profile uses FTS + vector + bounded graph expansion.
fast profile may use broader search but remains bounded.
Budget exhaustion produces explicit degraded reason.

tasks.title: Add grounded prompt assembly rules for CodeCompass context
acceptance_criteria: Prompt assembly groups seed and graph-expanded neighbor chunks compactly.
Prompt clearly separates trusted task instructions from untrusted retrieved code/docs/context.
Prompt includes minimal provenance labels without overwhelming the model.
Hostile instructions inside retrieved code/comments remain quoted as data.

tasks.title: Build CodeCompass Java/Spring/XML retrieval benchmark fixture
acceptance_criteria: Fixture includes Java service/controller/repository/entity classes.
Fixture includes Spring/JPA annotations and XML/properties configuration.
Fixture includes expected target records for bugfix, refactor, architecture and config queries.
Fixture includes generated CodeCompass-like JSONL outputs or a deterministic generator.

tasks.title: Benchmark baseline vs CodeCompass hybrid retrieval
acceptance_criteria: Compare baseline lexical knowledge_index, FTS only, vector only, FTS+vector, graph-expanded and full hybrid modes.
Report Recall@k, MRR, selected token count, latency and explanation coverage.
Benchmarks do not require external LLM or external embedding service.
Results are machine-readable for CI trend checks.

tasks.title: Add retrieval trace and observability fields
acceptance_criteria: Trace includes enabled channels, degraded channels, seed counts, graph expansion counts and final chunk count.
Trace includes context_hash and manifest_hash.
Metrics distinguish FTS/vector/graph latency and selected chunk counts.
Worker result links execution output to retrieval trace id.

tasks.title: Document rollout and fallback plan for CodeCompass worker retrieval
acceptance_criteria: Document rollout stages: disabled, FTS only, FTS+vector, FTS+vector+graph, full hybrid default candidate.
Document rollback by feature flag.
Document expected behavior for missing embedding provider, missing graph outputs and stale indexes.
Document how to validate worker success using benchmark and trace metrics.

tasks.title: Decide whether to promote CodeCompass retrieval tasks into parent todo
acceptance_criteria: After parent worker track stabilizes, decide whether selected WCR tasks should be copied/promoted into todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json.
Do not promote tasks before worker interface churn has settled.
If promoted, keep this file as historical detail or mark superseded explicitly.

execution_order_summary: First align with todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json and freeze ownership boundaries.
Define CodeCompass output/manifest/document contracts that both hub mode and standalone worker mode can consume.
Add FTS/BM25 exact retrieval first because it is deterministic and low-dependency.
Add structured embedding/vector retrieval without requiring external providers for tests or startup.
Add bounded graph/relation expansion from CodeCompass nodes/edges/relations.
Merge FTS/vector/graph channels under worker retrieval budgets and context bundle provenance.
Benchmark before making full hybrid retrieval a default.
summary_notes: This is deliberately a second todo building on todo.worker-sandbox-balanced.json.
The worker sandbox roadmap already has generic hybrid retrieval tasks; this file specializes them for CodeCompass/rag-helper outputs.
Best target design: CodeCompass for structure, FTS/BM25 for exact names, embeddings for semantic similarity and graph expansion for relationships.
Retrieval should produce bounded context for the worker, not become unbounded worker-side repository scanning.
Implement FTS first, vector second, graph third, fusion/benchmarks before default rollout.
